name: Mazdutide
subtitle: Dual GLP-1/Glucagon Receptor Agonist | Weight Loss & Diabetes

molecular.type: Oxyntomodulin analog with fatty acid conjugation
modifications: C20 fatty diacid conjugation via hydrophilic linker at Lys20 for extended half-life

overview: First-in-class dual GLP-1 and glucagon receptor agonist combining appetite suppression with thermogenesis stimulation. Phase 3 trials demonstrated superiority over semaglutide for weight loss and glycemic control.
mechanism: Dual agonist activation: GLP-1 stimulates insulin, suppresses glucagon, slows gastric emptying; Glucagon increases energy expenditure and thermogenesis while GLP-1 counteracts glucose-raising effects.
keyBenefits: Increased energy expenditure via glucagon receptor activation
Improved cardiometabolic markers (BP, lipids, liver fat)

items.name: Severe Obesity Management
items.description: GLORY-2 demonstrated 20.1% weight loss with 9mg over 60 weeks; 48.7% achieved ≥20% reduction.

items.name: Metabolic Syndrome Improvement
items.description: Significant reductions in waist circumference, systolic BP (-7.57 mmHg), triglycerides (-43%).

items.name: Liver Fat Reduction
items.description: Exploratory analysis showed 80.2% reduction in liver fat, suggesting MASLD/MASH benefits.

items.name: Glycemic Control
items.description: HbA1c reductions of 1.41-2.03% across trials; DREAMS-3 showed -2.03% vs semaglutide -1.84%.

items.name: Dual Endpoint Achievement
items.description: 48% achieved HbA1c <7.0% AND ≥10% weight loss versus 21% with semaglutide.

items.name: Blood Pressure Reduction
items.description: Systolic reduction of -7.57 mmHg, diastolic -2.98 mmHg in clinical trials.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection delivering dual GLP-1/glucagon agonism with once-weekly dosing enabled by fatty acid conjugation.

steps: Allow vial to reach room temperature (15-20 minutes)
Clean vial top with alcohol swab, air dry completely
Calculate reconstitution volume for desired concentration
Draw bacteriostatic water into syringe, remove air bubbles
Inject BAC water slowly down vial side—drop by drop to prevent foaming
Gently swirl vial in circular motion
Verify complete clarity and colorless appearance
Label vial with reconstitution date and concentration

interactions.notes: Both are GLP-1 agonists; combining increases hypoglycemia and severe GI side effect risk.
Both target GLP-1 receptor; additive effects on GI symptoms and hypoglycemia risk.
Another GLP-1 agonist; dual therapy contraindicated due to additive receptor activation.
May require significant dose reduction due to improved glucose control; monitor for hypoglycemia.
Increased hypoglycemia risk; dose reduction may be necessary.
Complementary mechanisms; clinical trials allowed stable metformin use with enhanced efficacy.
No known interactions; may support gut health and potentially reduce GI side effects.
Delayed gastric emptying may affect absorption; separate administration by 1 hour.

timeline.effects: Appetite reduction, possible mild nausea, decreased portion sizes
Early weight loss begins (1-2%), improved satiety after meals
Dose escalation phase, GI symptoms typically improving, 3-5% weight loss
Steady weight loss continues (7-12%), energy expenditure effects evident
Significant weight reduction (12-17%), metabolic markers improving
Peak effects (up to 20% weight loss), sustained improvements in BP, lipids, glucose

stopSigns: Severe or persistent abdominal pain (potential pancreatitis)
Neck lumps, hoarseness, or difficulty swallowing
Severe nausea/vomiting preventing adequate nutrition or hydration
Signs of severe hypoglycemia (confusion, sweating, shakiness)
Severe allergic reactions (rash, difficulty breathing, facial swelling)
Unusual mood changes, depression, or suicidal thoughts
Signs of gallbladder problems (severe upper right abdominal pain)
contraindications: Personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma
MEN2 syndrome history (class warning for GLP-1 agonists)
monitoring: Start with lowest dose, escalate gradually every 4 weeks
Monitor blood glucose if taking insulin or sulfonylureas
Maintain adequate hydration during initial weeks

good: White to off-white lyophilized powder without clumping or discoloration
Completely clear and colorless appearance after reconstitution—no visible particles
Intact vial seal and clear labeling with mg dosage, batch numbers, expiration dates
warning: Source verification critical—available from research chemical suppliers
bad: Clumping, discoloration, or moisture in powder
Persistent cloudiness or particles after reconstitution indicates degradation

references.title: GLORY-1 Phase 3 Trial
references.keyFindings: 610 Chinese adults with obesity/overweight; 48 weeks. First Phase 3 weight management trial showing clinically meaningful weight reductions. NEJM.

references.title: GLORY-2 Phase 3 Trial
references.keyFindings: 462 Chinese adults with obesity; 60 weeks; 9mg dose. 20.1% weight loss vs 2.8% placebo; 48.7% achieved ≥20% reduction; no plateau observed.

references.title: DREAMS-3 Phase 3 Trial - Head-to-Head vs Semaglutide
references.keyFindings: 349 Chinese adults with T2D and obesity; 32 weeks. Mazdutide superiority: 48% vs 21% achieved HbA1c <7.0% AND ≥10% weight reduction (p<0.0001).

references.title: Phase 2 T2D Trial
references.keyFindings: Chinese adults with T2D; 20 weeks. HbA1c reductions 1.41-1.67% vs 0.03% placebo; weight loss up to 7.1%. Diabetes Care.

quickStats.typicalDose: Start 1.5-3mg weekly, titrate up to 6-9mg based on response
quickStats.cycleDuration: 48-60+ weeks for optimal results per clinical trials
quickStats.storage: Refrigerate reconstituted solution at 2-8°C, use within 30 days; lyophilized powder at -20°C until reconstitution
